description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the lighter, speckled stone material that makes up the rest of the block. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, with a uniform dark coloration, suggesting a dense and heavy construction. The top surface appears slightly rough and matte, with a natural stone texture visible between the grooves. The block is thick and robust, likely designed to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. There are no visible markings, engravings, or signs of significant weathering on the surface.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and solid, dark sides. The block has a rough, speckled stone texture and a heavy, robust appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped, grooved top surface and the solid, dark front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the block's thickness clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the parallel grooves and the solid back face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep grooves and the block's substantial thickness.
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained.
